title: StageSelector — Clip Development Stage Picker
problem: A band tracks each clip's creative stage (idea / sketch / developing / demo-ready) to know which songs are close to done vs. just started. This needs to be changeable from both the clip list (quick update inline) and the clip detail page without navigating.
analysis:
StageSelector renders the current stage as a tappable badge/chip. On tap → opens a BottomSheet with four stage options (idea, sketch, developing, demo-ready), current stage highlighted in amber. Tapping a stage saves via PATCH /api/sessions/[sessionId]/clips/[clipId] and dismisses the sheet.

Available to Recorder and Editor roles. Member role sees a static badge (same visual, no tap response).

Used in: ClipCard (compact chip), ClipDetailClient (in Team tab or actions section).
successCriteria:
- Stage badge tappable by Recorder/Editor, static for Member
- Tap → BottomSheet with 4 options, current highlighted in amber
- Select → optimistic update + PATCH to Postgres + sheet dismiss
- Stage chip colors: idea (muted), sketch (blue-dim), developing (amber-dim), demo-ready (green-dim)
- Accessible: aria-label 'Change stage' on button, aria-checked on active option
implementation: components/clips/StageSelector.tsx: props { clipId, sessionId, currentStage, canEdit, onStageChange? }. Internal state: sheetOpen. Renders badge-button if canEdit; static Badge if not. BottomSheet contains 4 option rows. On select: call PATCH API → update state → onStageChange callback for parent to refresh.

decisions.decision: Stage change happens in a bottom sheet, not an inline dropdown
decisions.rationale: An inline dropdown (select element) on a dark mobile UI is hard to style consistently across browsers. A BottomSheet provides native-feeling interaction with full control over appearance, matching the app's bottom-sheet-for-all-choices pattern.
decisions.recommendation: BottomSheet with 4 full-width option rows. No select element used.

knownLimitations: Stage change is not versioned — no history of stage transitions stored
userGuide: Tap the stage chip (Idea, Sketch, Developing, Demo-Ready) on any clip to update where the song is in the creative process. Changes are visible to all band members immediately.
